description_long: This object is a collection of twelve axes, each featuring a robust wooden handle and a dark metallic blade. The handles are straight to slightly curved, with a natural wood grain texture and reinforced with metal bands near the blade for added durability. The blades vary in shape, including classic single-bevel, double-bevel, and some with decorative or tactical cutouts, as well as different edge profiles such as straight, curved, and serrated. The metal heads are matte gray to black, with some axes featuring additional details like spikes, beards, or hammer polls. The overall design suggests these axes are intended for both utility and display, with a rugged, hand-forged appearance. No visible branding or markings are present on any of the axes.
description: A set of twelve axes with wooden handles and dark metal blades, each featuring unique blade shapes and reinforced grips. The axes display a rugged, hand-forged look with various edge profiles and tactical details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full profiles of all twelve axes, highlighting the diversity in blade shapes and handle designs.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thinness of the blades and handles, with minimal detail visible except the edge alignment.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the backs of the axe heads and the curvature of the handles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the slim profiles of the axes and the alignment of the blades.
attribute_handle_type: Straight to slightly curved wooden handles with visible grain, reinforced near the blade, oval cross-section, no pronounced grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Varied blade shapes including single and double-bevel, some with beards, spikes, or serrated edges; matte dark metal finish, some tactical cutouts.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and pinned mounting with visible metal bands securing the blade to the handle.
